member.name: Barry Gardiner
member.synopsis: Barry Gardiner is the Labour MP for Brent West , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 1997.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she agrees with Recommendation 46 of the Independent Water Commission.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our White Paper commits to taking forward Recommendation 46 of the IWC. We will make it easier for a company’s owners to change their ownership model, by ensuring the new regulator has a transparent process to assess whether an ownership change should go ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has made an assessment of the potential merits of the inclusion of Recommendation 46 of the Independent Water Commission in full in the Water White Paper.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Our White Paper commits to taking forward Recommendation 46 of the IWC. We will make it easier for a company’s owners to change their ownership model, by ensuring the new regulator has a transparent process to assess whether an ownership change should go ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Thames Water: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to UIN 5852, what advice her department has received from the ONS regarding the implications for ONS classification if Thames Water was taken into Special Administration.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury has not asked for, nor received, any advice from the ONS regarding the implications for ONS classification if Thames Water was taken into Special Administration.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what discussions her Department has had with the NAO on the use of the Special Administration Regime in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government and the National Audit Office regularly engage in discussions on economic issues as part of ongoing stakeholder relations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what discussions her Department has had with the NAO on the use of the Special Administration Regime in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury has not held discussions with the National Audit Office (NAO) on the use of the Special Administration Regime in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what discussions her department has had with the ONS regarding the use of the Special Administration Regime in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury and the Office for National Statistics (ONS) regularly discuss matters in the UK economy which could affect ONS classification decisions, as part of regular stakeholder engagement.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Water
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department plans to designate bathing water status to the 2,487 recreationally-used freshwater sites identified by the Environment Agency which currently do not have such s
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government designates bathing waters following a public application and consultation process. This means the process is driven by individuals and groups with direct ties to sites highly popular with bathers, who best understand how they are used and v...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Insolvency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what discussions her department has had with the ONS regarding the use of the Special Administration Regime in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Statistics Authority (which includes the ONS) is a Non Ministerial Department sponsored by the Cabinet Office. At present, DEFRA has not had discussions with the ONS regarding the use of SAR in the water sector.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Climate Change
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has plans to meet with the Climate Change Committee to discuss the potential impact of climate change on the schools’ estate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department welcomes the Climate Change Committee’s Well-Adapted UK report which highlights risks to critical infrastructure including schools. Departmental officials will be attending engagement sessions with the Climate Change Committee throughout 20...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Assessments: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she is taking steps to mitigate the potential impact of hot weather on examination results.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises the challenges posed by high temperatures during the exam period and has published guidance to support schools in managing hot weather available at: https://educationhub.blog.gov.uk/2025/06/hot-weather-and-heatwaves-guidance-for-...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Public Consultation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, when the National Policy Statement on data centres will be published for consultation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that data centres facilitate the AI development and application that will be critical to the UK’s economic growth, resilience and technological leadership, and is taking coordinated action across planning, energy and digital infr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Carbon Emissions: Data Centres
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether the Government plans to take steps to balance its updated emissions estimates of data centres with the decarbonisation required in the fifth carbon budget.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Growth in data centre electricity demand is inherently uncertain and, where additional electricity demand does materialise, the UK’s rapidly decarbonising power sector will help to mitigate the emissions impact. We will continue to test a range of data ce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what her Department's policy is on operator self-monitoring.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are putting an end to Operator Self-Monitoring and ensuring the public have confidence in the data that is produced from water company assets. We will implement an Open Monitoring approach so everyone can see what is happening, with clear, accessible d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Thames Water
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what the principal statutory duties are of Thames Water Utilities Limited's licence.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under section 37 of the Water Industry Act 1991 a water company has the duty to ensure the supply of water within its area of appointment and to develop and maintain an efficient and economical supply system for that purpose, as well as to ensure arrangem...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the Answer of 21 April to Question 127788 on Water Companies: Prosecutions, of the 37 completed prosecutions against Water and Sewerage Companies since 27 March 2020, how m
writtenQuestions.answerText: Of the 37 prosecutions since 27 March 2020, 32 prosecutions were for offences that took place before 27 March 2020, and 5 prosecutions were for offences that took place after 27 March 2020.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when she plans to answer Written Parliamentary Question UIN 124051.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Written Parliamentary Question UIN 124051 was answered on 28 April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs: Written Questions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, when she plans to answer Written Parliamentary Question UIN 123113.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Written Parliamentary Question UIN 123113 was answered on 16 April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many prosecutions the Environment Agency has completed against Water and Sewerage Companies for offences that have taken place since 27 March 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 27 March 2020, there have been 37 completed prosecutions against Water and Sewerage Companies. A water quality prosecution is any criminal proceedings relating to a failure to properly manage water or sewage treatment.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Sewage: Waste Disposal
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, since 27 March 2020, how many offences relating to discharge of sewage from a water company asset have the Environment Agency prosecuted.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 27 March 2020, there have been 37 completed prosecutions against Water and Sewerage Companies. A water quality prosecution is any criminal proceedings relating to a failure to properly manage water or sewage treatment.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the Answer of to Question 122633, on Water Companies: Repairs and Maintenance, whether her Department will publish the initial cost information on ending operator self-moni
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed to ending Operator Self Monitoring. The Environment Agency has provided early, high‑level indicative cost estimates to support the Department’s initial exploration of options for future monitoring arrangements. These figures w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

Unremunerated director of GLOBE International AISBL, an international non-profit network devoted to legislative leadership on sustainable development and climate change. Date interest arose: 7 January 2016 (Registered 3 February 2016)

Trustee of Climate Parliament, which aims to educate and inform legislators about climate change and policy options for promoting renewable energy. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 4 February 2019 (Registered 28 February 2019)

A Member and Director of Stop Fire and Rehire Ltd, a company limited by guarantee set up to manage financial support for my Private Members Bill campaign. The company is a not for profit company and the position is entirely unpaid. Date interest arose: 6 July 2021 (Registered 10 September 2021)

A Member and Director of Together 2030, a company limited by guarantee. The company is a not for profit company and the position is entirely unpaid. Date interest arose: 18 November 2021 (Registered 2 December 2021)
